title: Star Tours (real-world)
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the original real world attraction|the [[Star Tours: The Adventures Continue|current version]] of the ride, the fictional company [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]], or the [[Star Tours: Taking Off on a Star Tour|''Star Wars Insider'' article]]}} {{Movie |image=[[File:1986StarToursPoster.jpg]] |title=''Star Tours'' |director=[[Tony Baxter]], [[Dennis Muren]]{{InsiderCite|104|Star Tours: Taking Off on a Star Tour|''Star Tours'': Taking Off on a Star Tour}} |producer=[[Tom Fitzgerald]], [[George Lucas]] |production company= |writer=Tom Fitzgerald, George Lucas |starring=*[[Anthony Daniels]] *[[Paul Reubens]] *[[Steve Gawley]] *Tom Fitzgerald *[[Mike West]] |music=[[Richard Bellis]], [[John Williams]] |release date=*[[January 9]], [[1987]] {{C|Disneyland}} *[[July 12]], [[1989]] {{C|Tokyo Disneyland}}{{ThisWeek|EU-glArwz-U|The Bad Batch Returns, Your Photos of The Child, and More!|sw_url=video/the-bad-batch-returns-your-photos-of-the-child-and-more}} *[[December 15]], 1989 {{C|Disney's Hollywood Studios}} *[[April 12]], [[1992]] {{C|Disneyland Paris}}{{ThisWeek|fp_XUth1hXs|LEGO A-Wing Starfighter Reveal, Star Wars: The Clone Wars Anthology Book Cover, and More!|sw_url=video/lego-a-wing-starfighter-reveal-star-wars-the-clone-wars-anthology-book-cover-and-more}} |runtime=4 min. 30 sec. |budget= |language= |timeline=[[4 ABY/Legends|4.3 ABY]]{{Blog|holocron|7|Death Star timeline}} |series= |preceded by= |followed by=''[[Star Tours: The Adventures Continue]]'' }} {{Quote|''Star Tours'' is one of the best spin-offs or spins-off to come from the world of Lucas.|[[Anthony Daniels]]|{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=133031&start=30|thread=Unknown Thread|nobackup=1}}}} '''''Star Tours''''' was a [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] [[Wikipedia:Simulator ride|simulator ride]] [[Wikipedia:Amusement park|theme park]] attraction located at [[Disneyland]] at [[Wikipedia:Disneyland Resort|Disneyland Resort]] in [[Wikipedia:California|California]], [[Disney's Hollywood Studios]] at [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney World Resort|Walt Disney World]] in [[Wikipedia:Florida|Florida]], [[Tokyo Disneyland]] at [[Wikipedia:Tokyo Disney Resort|Tokyo Disney Resort]] in [[Wikipedia:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[Wikipedia:Japan|Japan]] and [[Disneyland Paris|Disneyland Park]] at [[Wikipedia:Disneyland Paris|Disneyland Paris]] in [[Wikipedia:Paris|Paris]], [[Wikipedia:France|France]]. Groups of visitors, or "passengers," were taken on by the fictional travel agency known as [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]], via a space tour vessel called the [[StarSpeeder 3000]] and set in the ''Star Wars'' universe. Thanks to an inexperienced and thoroughly incompetent [[Droid/Legends|droid]] pilot named [[RX-24/Legends|RX-24]], what was billed as a leisurely tour to the [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] moon became a wild ride as the tour got caught up in a battle between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]]. The first incarnation of the ride appeared in Tomorrowland at [[Wikipedia:Disneyland|Disneyland]] in [[1987]], replacing the attraction [[Wikipedia:Adventure Thru Inner Space|''Adventure Thru Inner Space'']]. The original ''Star Tours'' closed in [[2010]] ([[July 27]] in Disneyland, [[September 8]] in Disney's Hollywood Studios) while Disney engineers built the updated and renovated ride, ''[[Star Tours: The Adventures Continue]]'', which opened on [[May 20]], [[2011]].{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/latestnews/story/Star_Tours_Returns_Starting_May_20th_136170.asp|text=Star Tours Returns Starting May 20th|author=Geller, Eric|date=2011-01-18|archivedate=20190729153331}} [[Wikipedia:Tokyo Disneyland|Tokyo Disneyland's]] version of the original ride closed in April [[2012]] to make way for the updated ride in Spring [[2013]].{{EndorExpress|url=star-wars-adventures/star-tours-2/2011/11/star-tours-the-adventures-continue-opening-in-tokyo-disneyland-spring-2013|text=Star Tours: The Adventures Continue opening in Tokyo Spring 2013}} Disneyland Paris's version closed on [[March 16]], [[2016]] to make way for the update.{{WebCite|url=https://dedicatedtodlp.com/the-vault/final-boarding-call-for-endor-express-saying-goodbye-to-star-tours-in-disneyland-paris|work=Dedicated to DLP|archivedate=20240718101152|text=Final Boarding Call For Endor Express – Saying Goodbye to Star Tours in Disneyland Paris}} Some footage from the original ride appeared years later as part of a flight simulator in the [[Star Wars: Millennium Falcon (attraction)|''Star Wars'': Millennium Falcon]] themed area of the ''Disney Dream'' cruise ship.{{YouTube|video=KnSIv6hnZl0|text=Classic Star Tours on the Millennium Falcon on the Disney Dream|channel=UC7SjQGMropNWcVKwtwVQkbg|name=The DIS}} ==Plot summary== [[File:Star_tours_logo.svg|250px|right|The ''Star Tours'' logo]] Advertised as "The Ultimate Star Wars Adventure!," ''Star Tours'' puts the guest in the role of a space tourist en route to the Forest Moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] via the "Star Tours" travel agency. Much is made of this throughout the ride queue, and the design and theme of the inside holding area is convincingly modeled to look like a spaceship boarding terminal. This area is stocked with [[Wikipedia:Audio-Animatronics|Audio-Animatronic]] characters that seem to interact with the ride patrons including [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] technicians and versions of [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], as well as a life-size mock-up of the StarSpeeder 3000, the "[[Starship/Legends|starship]]" that guests embark on. The figures of C-3PO and R2-D2 in the Disneyland park are actual props from the original film trilogy, modified to operate via [[Wikipedia:Audio-Animatronics|Audio-Animatronics]].Trahan, Kendra. ''Disneyland Detective: An Independent Guide to Discovering Disney's Legend, Lore, and Magic''. Permagrin Publishing (2004). Once guests reach the head of the line, the ride operators escort them into one of several ride theaters. As the doors close, the ship's bumbling [[RX-24/Legends|RX-24]] pilot [[Droid/Legends|droid]], a.k.a. "Rex" (voiced by [[Paul Reubens]]), chats up the guests about the trip as he sets up. All goes well until a slight mistake on Rex's part sends the ship down the wrong tunnel and plummeting into a maintenance yard, just managing to escape into open space before a giant mechanical appendage nearly crushes the ship. Once in space, the ship enters [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], but Rex disengages its [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] too late to stop at the ship's intended destination—and instead gets caught inside a [[Comet/Legends|comet]] field. The ship becomes trapped inside one of the larger comets and has to navigate its way out through a maze of passages and chambers. Just when all the trouble seems to be over, the ship encounters a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] and finds itself caught in its tractor beam. However, a timely attack (providing assistance by destroying the tractor beam generator) by a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] fighter allows them to escape. Soon the ship and its tourists find themselves accompanying the Rebellion on a massive assault on the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] (which Death Star is unspecified). Rex uses the StarSpeeder's lasers to eliminate [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] while a Rebel destroys the Death Star in the same manner as [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] does in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. A final light-speed jump sends the StarSpeeder back to where it started, but not before a near-collision with a [[fuel truck]] in the spaceport. ==Development== [[File:Starspeeder_3000_specifications.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Technical specifications for the StarSpeeder 3000 (from the [[Wikipedia:Disney's Hollywood Studios|Disney's Hollywood Studios]] version of the ride)]] The ride that became ''Star Tours'' first saw light as a proposal for an attraction based on the [[1979]] Disney live-action flop [[Wikipedia:The Black Hole|''The Black Hole'']]. It was planned as an interactive ride simulator attraction, where guests would have had the ability to choose the ride car's route. After preliminary planning, the ''Black Hole'' attraction was shelved due to its enormous cost (approximately $50 million US) as well as the unpopularity of the film itself.{{EndorExpress|url=star-wars-adventures/star-tours/creating-star-tours/2009/08/creating-fantasies|text=Creating Fantasies}} With his approval, [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney Imagineering|Disney Imagineers]] purchased four large military flight simulators at a cost of $500,000 each and designed the ride structure. Meanwhile, Lucas and his team of special-effects technicians at [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM) worked on the first-person-perspective film that would be projected inside the simulators. When both simulator and film were completed, a programmer then sat inside and, with the aid of a joystick, manually synchronized the movement of the simulator with the apparent movement on the film. On January 9, 1987, at a final cost of $32 million (almost twice the cost of building the entire park in 1955), the ride finally opened to throngs of patrons, many of whom dressed as ''Star Wars'' characters for the occasion. In celebration, Disneyland remained open for a special 60-hour marathon from 10:00am on January 9, 1987 to 10:00pm on January 11, 1987. ===Sequel=== The flight-information board in the ride queue promised forthcoming adventures to [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]] and displays the text "Soon Endor Won't Be The End-All." These details, especially the tagline, were often mistaken for recent additions, used to support rumors of impending changes to the attraction, but have actually been in place since ''Star Tours'' opened.{{YouTube|video=_Cs5OoS086o|text=Disneyland Star Tours queuing in Line 2008|channel=UCZ6FAOsQH9ncEra5hdrDrQQ|name=xurbition}} In [[April]] [[2005]], at the ''Star Wars'' [[Celebration III]], ''Star Wars'' creator George Lucas confirmed that ''[[Star Tours: The Adventures Continue]]'' was in production.{{SW|url=news/5-of-the-best-reveals-in-star-wars-celebration-history|text=5 of the Best Reveals in ''Star Wars'' Celebration History}} This new ride is set in the time between [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|Episode III]] and Episode IV. The sequel includes 54 different story combinations and travel to a number of planets from the entire film saga. The new ride system features a 3D film and an improved motion simulator.{{InsiderCite|127|The Incredible Journey}} The sequel opened on [[May 20]], 2011 at Walt Disney World{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/latestnews/story/Star_Tours_Returns_Starting_May_20th_136170.asp|text=Star Tours Returns Starting May 20th|author=Geller, Eric|date=2011-01-18|archivedate=20190729153331}}, on [[June 3]] at Disneyland{{DPB|url=2011/02/from-under-the-sea-to-galaxies-far-far-away-opening-dates-are-set-for-a-soundsational-summer-at-disneyland-resort|text=From Under the Sea to Galaxies Far, Far Away…Opening Dates Are Set For A Soundsational Summer at Disneyland Resort}}, on [[May 7]], 2013 at Tokyo Disneyland{{EndorExpress|url=parks/disneyparks/tokyo-disneyland/2013/05/konnichiwa-star-tours|text=Konnichiwa, Star Tours!}}, and in Paris on [[March 18]], [[2017]].{{WebCite|url=https://radiodisneyclub.fr/star-tours-2-disneyland-paris|text=Star Tours : The Adventure Continues à Disneyland Paris pour les 25 ans|author=Gauthier, Kevin|date=2014-10-07|language=French|archivedate=20190608192814}} ==Attraction facts {{SectionFlag}}== [[File:StarToursEntrance96_wb.jpg|thumb|250px|Disneyland ''Star Tours'' entrance in 1996 before Tomorrowland makeover]] *'''Designer:''' Walt Disney Imagineering, Industrial Light and Magic *'''Simulators:''' 4 {{C|Disneyland}}; 6 {{C|Disney Hollywood Studios, Tokyo Disneyland and Disneyland Paris}} *'''Guests per simulator:''' 40 *'''Height requirement:''' 40 inches (102 cm){{Disney|subdomain=disneyland|domain=go.com|url=disneyland/en_US/parks/attractions/detail?name=StarToursAttractionPage|text=Disneyland: Star Tours|nolive=1}} *'''Sponsors:''' **Disneyland: [[Wikipedia:M&M's|M&M's]] (1987–1995), [[Wikipedia:Energizer Holdings|Energizer]] (1995–2006) **Tokyo Disneyland: [[Wikipedia:Panasonic|Panasonic]] (1999–2009) **Disneyland Paris: [[Wikipedia:IBM|IBM]] (1992–2002) **Walt Disney World: [[Wikipedia:M&M's|M&M's]] (1989–1995), [[Wikipedia:Energizer Holdings|Energizer]] (1995–2006) *'''Show length:''' 4:30 *'''Ride system:''' Flight simulator with Audio-Animatronics, synced to film ==Marketing {{SectionFlag}}== [[File:Star_Tours.jpg|thumb|left|250px|''Star Tours'' at Disney's Hollywood Studios]] When the ride opened, ''Star Tours'' press kits were released. They each contained interviews with George Lucas, Disney's [[Wikipedia:Michael Eisner|Michael Eisner]], and C-3PO. The video portion of the kit lasts about an hour, with a large part of it devoted to the ride's opening ceremony, and a play with actors dressed as [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]—dancing around and acting out several scenes from the ''Star Wars'' films.{{YouTube|video=cfGBk-BXq-0|text=Star Tours Press Kit VHS|channel=UCQPq8r6I4OVQb0QITPiAVDQ|name=The Imperial Academy}} There was also a TV special that aired around the time of the opening of the ride. The program—entitled [[George Lucas' Star Tours|George Lucas' ''Star Tours'']] and designed to promote the ride—aired in late [[1986]], and was hosted by [[Wikipedia:Gil Gerard|Gil Gerard]] and [[Wikipedia:Ernie Reyes Jr.|Ernie Reyes Jr.]]. The show looks at how ''Star Tours'' was made, as well as the history of space travel and space-related films. Some highlights of the program include a segment at the beginning, where C-3PO and R2-D2 make an appearance and perform a rap song, as well as a segment at the end, where Reyes boards the StarSpeeder 3000—giving away some teasers of the ride itself.[[George Lucas' Star Tours|George Lucas' ''Star Tours'']] As part of the ''Star Tours'' experience, upon exiting the StarSpeeder 3000, passengers are led to a store which sells merchandise based on ''Star Wars'' and the ride itself. This includes action figures, clothing, etc. At Disneyland the store is [[The Star Trader]].{{D23|url=a-to-z/star-traders|text=Disney A to Z: Star Trader}} At Disney's Hollywood Studios, the store was originally called [[Tatooine Traders|Endor Vendors]], and had a theme based on Endor with a facade modeled after the back door of the [[Endor shield generator bunker]]. Around the release of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I]], that store was replaced by [[Tatooine Traders]], themed to resemble the buildings of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] and [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]].{{D23|url=a-to-z/tatooine-traders|text=Disney A to Z: Tatooine Traders}} Both stores included some [[Star Wars: Star Tours (toy line)|exclusive merchandise]] sold only at Disney theme parks, including action figures of the various droids seen in the ride and queue and StarSpeeder 3000 toys.{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Star Tours|text=''Star Tours'' toy line|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/startours.asp}} ==Continuity== [[File:StarToursEntrance98_wb.jpg|thumb|250px|Disneyland ''Star Tours'' entrance in 1998 after makeover]] Initially a date for the events of the original ''Star Tours'' was not specified. Press materials at the time of the attraction's opening did place it after the events of ''Return of the Jedi'',{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/banthatracks/bt35/index.html|text=Bantha Tracks #35}} while C-3PO's dialogue refers to his visits to various planets as happening in the past, including the events of his capture by the Ewoks. The ''[[Complete Guide to Star Tours]]'' booklet made available at Tokyo Disneyland further confirmed the setting.''[[Complete Guide to Star Tours]]'' Some were led to question whether or not the ride was canon in the ''Star Wars'' universe, mostly due to the inclusion of an attack on the Death Star, including a trench run similar to that seen in {{Film|IV}}. Though early press material suggested the space station was a new Death Star appearing after ''Return of the Jedi'',{{Starlog|118|Star Wars: The Disneyland Journey, Part One|''Star Wars'': The Disneyland Journey, Part One}} no sources that followed offered an explanation for that station for many years. In 2005, [[Lucasfilm]]'s [[Leland Chee]] first used the term "Death Star III" and placed its destruction a few months after the Battle of Endor in a blog post.{{Blog|holocron|7|Death Star timeline}} This was later confirmed as canon in the article [[Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours]] on StarWars.com, though no explanation was given for the station. That article also set the events of the ride during the [[Nagai–Tof War]], shown to be happening months after ''Return of the Jedi'' in the [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'' comic series]].{{SW|url=news/convenient-daily-departures-the-history-of-star-tours|text=Convenient Daily Departures: The History of ''Star Tours''|int=Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours|nolive=1}} Some fans had speculated that the Death Star could have been modification of one of the [[habitation sphere]]s seen under construction over [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. This was later confirmed in [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Ennix Devian]]'s entry for the second part of the blog series [[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]].{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2|serieslink=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} As with all material released prior to [[April 25]], [[2014]], questions of canonicity regarding ''Star Tours'' apply only to the continuity of the Expanded Universe, now known as [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]], and its story is considered entirely non-canon within the current continuity.{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}} ===Legends references=== [[File:STours.png|thumb|250px|Map with some of the locations Star Tours operated on, including Earth]] The Expanded Universe (now known as Legends) made a number of references to the fictional travel agency and its ships: *Several aliens from the ''Star Wars'' theatrical films and offshoot productions make appearances in the opening safety video: Some [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], a [[Gran/Legends|Gran]], a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] (a [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] costume), and [[Teek (Teek)|Teek]] from ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]''. *In the PC game [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''TIE Fighter'']], in the first TIE Interceptor historical mission, when the player uses sensors to identify a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]] its name reads "[[StarTour 7]]," and its cargo is listed as "Tourists."[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] *The StarSpeeder 3000 has been referenced in the novel ''[[Specter of the Past]]''.''[[Specter of the Past]]'' *The article ''[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]'' in [[Star Wars Gamer 9|''Star Wars Gamer'' 9]] explains that a short-lived travel agency offered trips to Endor before accidents and mismanagement drove them out of business. It also suggests that those trips happened in the [[New Republic era]]. The article includes an illustration of a StarSpeeder 3000 at a landing site on Endor.{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}} *In the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' game, players encounter the crash site of the Star Tours ship ''[[Tzarina]]'' on Dathomir. The crash is blamed on an incompetent droid captain who got too close to what sounds like the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].{{GalaxiesNGE}} *The ride's [[G2 repair droid/Legends|G2 repair droids]] are featured in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' and are mentioned in the novel ''Specter of the Past''. ===Canon references=== *"[[Lightsaber Lost]]," an episode of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' animated series, includes a holographic advertisement from Star Tours promoting travel to [[Glee Anselm/Legends|Glee Anselm]].{{TCW|Lightsaber Lost}} *"[[Droids in Distress]]," an episode of the ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' animated series, features the heroes traveling in a civilian transport vessel rather similar to the StarSpeeder 3000, piloted by a droid of the same name and design as [[RX-24/Legends|RX-24]]; Paul Reubens reprised his role as the voice of Rex.{{Rebels|Droids in Distress}} *[[Oga's Cantina]], a dining establishment and attraction at [[Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge|''Star Wars'': Galaxy's Edge]] at Disneyland and Walt Disney World, features Rex, now reprogrammed to be a DJ and renamed [[R-3X]].{{GEAttr|Oga's Cantina}} The canon version of Rex also piloted a StarSpeeder 3000 for Star Tours at some point.{{Databank|dj-r-3x|DJ R-3X}} ==Cameos {{SectionFlag}}== [[File:Star_Tours_-_Muren_Eddy_Mullaly_cameo.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Muren, Eddy, and Mullaly]] Many background characters seen in the pre-flight videos and the ride film itself are portrayed by ILM employees who worked on the attraction. In the pre-ride video, modelmaker [[William Beck|Bill Beck]] is a technician performing the final pre-flight walk-around of the StarSpeeder. The passengers seen inside the speeder in the safety video are Imagineers and their families.{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/kessel/f20040618/index.html|series=Kessel Mines|text=A Look Back at Star Tours}} [[File:Star_Tours_Ira_Keeler_EE.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Ira Keeler]] Visual effects supervisor [[Dennis Muren]], camera operator [[Selwyn Eddy]] and modelmaker [[Claudia Mullaly]] appear at the beginning of the ride as crew members working in the maintenance area of the starport when RX-24 nearly crashes the ship into their office, sending the trio diving for cover behind a desk. Another worker seen ducking behind a desk before Rex crashes into a fuel truck towards the ride's end was played by modelmaker [[Ira Keeler]] (the character was in the past erroneously identified as being played by George Lucas). The more prominent character of X-wing pilot [[Red Leader (Star Tours)|Red Leader]], seen shouting a warning to the speeder on the in-cabin video screen, was played by model shop supervisor [[Steve Gawley]].{{BookCite|author=Vaz, Mark Cotta|container=|title=Industrial Light & Magic : into the digital realm|publisher=Del Rey|date=1996|link=https://archive.org/details/industriallightm0000vazm}} ==Easter eggs {{SectionFlag}}== ===Ride queue=== [[File:Kermit_The_Frog_Star_Tours.jpg|thumb|Kermit The Frog Droid]] *A number of inside jokes can be heard over the public-address system in the ride's queue:{{D23|url=9-out-of-this-world-facts-about-star-tours-30-years-of-continued-adventures|text=9 Out of This World Facts about Star Tours' Over 30 Years of Continued Adventures}} **The announcer calls out for an illegally parked speeder with license number THX [[1138 (easter egg)|1138]], which is the name of the first studio film made by George Lucas. **The announcer pages a "Mr. [[Egroeg Sacul]]." The name is "George Lucas" spelled backwards. **A voice says there is a message for "Mr. [[Tom Morrow]]," who was a character in Disneyland's now-defunct ''Flight to the Moon'' attraction and later became a separate character for [[Wikipedia:Innoventions|Innoventions]] at Epcot and Disneyland Park in 1998. *The StarSpeeder 3000 has several blast marks, one of which appears to resemble a [[Wikipedia:Hidden Mickey|Hidden Mickey]]. *The G2 repair droids in the queue line are actually the skeletons of [[Wikipedia:Audio-Animatronics|Audio-Animatronic]] geese from an old Disneyland show, [[Wikipedia:America Sings|''America Sings'']]. They were removed from the show during the last two years of its run. The droids can be heard singing "I've been working on the same droid, all the live-long day," a reference to the geese singing "[[Wikipedia:I've Been Working on the Railroad|I've Been Working on the Railroad]]" during ''America Sings''. ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' nicknamed the G2s "Goose droids" as a nod to this show. *In the queue, you can see a model of [[Wikipedia:Kermit the Frog|Kermit the Frog]] made out of spare parts when you get to the overhead conveyor belt.{{BookCite|author=Veness, Susan|title=The Hidden Magic of Walt Disney World|publisher=Adams Media|date=2009|isbn=1-60550-063-1|link=https://archive.org/details/hiddenmagicofwal0000vene}} *The baskets of parts in the Droidnostics Center in the ride queue at Disneyland and Disney's Hollywood Studios have hidden initials and birth dates of Walt Disney Imagineering and ILM team members who worked on the attraction. *The woman sitting next to Teek in the preshow video is wearing a cast member costume for EPCOT Computer Central, an attraction formerly located in Epcot's [[Wikipedia:CommuniCore|CommuniCore]] pavilion.{{WebCite|url=http://www.imagineeringdisney.com/blog/2012/9/13/epcot-30th-a-photo-tour-of-the-past-part-1.html|work=Imagineering Disney Blog|archivedate=20240526001938|text=EPCOT 30TH: A Photo Tour of the Past [Part 1]}} *The exit of the Tokyo Disneyland version features a "[[Tourscan]]" kiosk that uses the footage from the multiple endings of the discontinued [[Wikipedia:Epcot|Epcot]] attraction [[Wikipedia:Horizons (Disney)|Horizons]] to portray other Star Tours excursions. The sets for the endings footage, and by extension the locales featured at the exit, were developed by [[David Jones]], who also did special-effects work for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].{{WebCite|url=http://progresscityusa.com/2011/09/01/the-horizons-story-part-iii-disneys-house-of-magic|text=The Horizons Story, Part III: Disney's House Of Magic|author=Crawford, Michael|date=2011-09-24|work=The Horizons Story|archivedate=20110924203238}} *One of the droids, [[DL-X2]], was a modified version of an Imagineer-developed prop for the robot TK4600 from the NBC police drama series [[Wikipedia:Hill Street Blues|''Hill Street Blues'']].{{BookCite|author=Newborn, Alex|container=Star Tours|title=A Long Time Ago: Exploring the Star Wars Cinematic Universe|publisher=Sequart Organization|date=2015}} ===Ride film=== *At Disneyland in Anaheim, California, ''Star Tours'' replaced an attraction known as ''Adventure Thru Inner Space'', in which guests were notionally shrunk to microscopic size. Exiting the first scene during the ''Star Tours'' film, the Mighty Microscope from the old ride can be seen. *On Captain Rex, a bright red tag can be seen attached to his torso. The tag says "Remove before Flight." This tag is a reference to similar labels placed on some aircraft parts. *Rex "[[I have a bad feeling about this|has a very bad feeling about this]]" when the ship flies into one of the comets. This is a [[Wikipedia:Running gag|running gag]] in the ''Star Wars'' films. *Rex is voiced by [[Paul Reubens]], best known for playing the character [[Wikipedia:Pee-wee Herman|Pee-wee Herman]]. Despite rumors that Reubens' voice was replaced following his 1991 or 2002 arrests, no such replacement occurred. One of the droids in the queue says, "You got a camera. Why don't you take a picture? It'll last longer!," which is a Paul Reubens quote. *During the ride, Rex says "I've always wanted to do this!" In "[[One Last Night in the Mos Eisley Cantina: The Tale of the Wolfman and the Lamproid]]," [[Lak Sivrak/Legends|Lak Sivrak]] hears a droid announce it had "always wanted to do this" during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].{{StoryCite|story=One Last Night in the Mos Eisley Cantina: The Tale of the Wolfman and the Lamproid|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}} *The registration number on the side of the fuel truck the speeder nearly crashes into is Lucasfilm's old office phone number. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Tours (real-world)}} ===Poster and advertising gallery {{SectionFlag}}=== File:1986StarToursPoster.jpg|Poster for Disneyland version File:Star_Tours_poster_Kriegler.jpg|Poster for Disneyland version File:Star_Tours_DL_print_ad.jpg|Print advertisement for Disneyland version File:Star_Tours_DL_print_ad_2.jpg|Print advertisement for Disneyland version File:StarTours-DisneyMGMStudiosPrintAd.jpg|Print advertisement for Disney-MGM Studios version File:Star_Tours_TDL_poster.jpg|Poster for Tokyo Disneyland version File:Star_Tours_DLP_poster.jpg|Poster for Disneyland Paris version ==Credits== {{Credits |cast= *[[Paul Reubens]] ....
